_brief: A web platform for high school students to manage and collaborate on school clubs. It centralizes member management, communication, event planning, and resources. Audience: students (13–18), club leaders, and faculty advisors. Objectives: streamline club operations, improve engagement and transparency, reduce administrative overhead, and provide compliant, secure access.
project_title: ClubHub: High School Club Management Platform
summary: A multi-tenant web platform for high school students, club leaders, and faculty advisors to manage club operations including member management, event planning, task coordination, budgets, and communication. The platform requires school SSO integration, FERPA/COPPA compliance, and integrations with Google/Microsoft ecosystems.

stories.title: Stakeholder interviews (students, advisors, admins)
stories.notes: Need access to actual high school stakeholders for validation

stories.title: Technical architecture design (multi-tenant, auth, integrations)
stories.notes: Multi-tenant design decisions will cascade to all features

stories.title: FERPA/COPPA compliance review with legal
stories.notes: External legal review required; may surface blocking requirements

stories.title: Design system and component library setup
Google Workspace SSO integration
stories.notes: Each school's Google Workspace config varies; expect debugging

stories.title: Microsoft AAD SSO integration
stories.notes: AAD tenant configurations are notoriously inconsistent

stories.title: Role-based access control system
stories.notes: Four user types × club-level permissions × custom permissions = complex matrix

stories.title: Audit logging infrastructure
stories.notes: Required for FERPA compliance

stories.title: Data isolation and tenant security
stories.notes: Critical for compliance; must be bulletproof

stories.title: Role assignment and custom permissions per club
Bulk import/export (CSV)
stories.notes: Data validation and error handling often underestimated

stories.title: Points/badges gamification system
stories.notes: Scope creep risk; needs clear rules definition

stories.title: Discussion threads with @mentions
stories.notes: Real-time updates add complexity

stories.title: Push notification infrastructure
stories.notes: PWA push notifications have browser-specific quirks

stories.title: Polls/voting with quorum and timebox
stories.notes: Edge cases: ties, late votes, quorum changes mid-poll

stories.title: File upload and object storage integration
Google Drive integration
stories.notes: OAuth scopes and school admin consent flows are complex

stories.title: Microsoft OneDrive integration

stories.title: Search indexing for documents
stories.notes: Full-text search across files requires dedicated infrastructure

stories.title: Event creation with RSVP, capacity, waitlists
stories.notes: Waitlist promotion logic needs careful design

stories.title: Google Calendar sync
stories.notes: Two-way sync is significantly harder than one-way

stories.title: Kanban task board
stories.notes: Drag-and-drop, real-time updates

stories.title: Task dependencies
stories.notes: Dependency graphs and blocking logic add significant complexity

stories.title: Data export for school admins
stories.notes: FERPA requires specific data handling

stories.title: Success metrics tracking implementation
stories.notes: Adoption, engagement, efficiency metrics require instrumentation

stories.title: WCAG 2.1 AA audit and remediation
stories.notes: Accessibility across all features; often 20-30% of UI effort

stories.title: PWA implementation (offline, install)
stories.notes: Service worker caching strategy needs careful design

stories.title: CDN setup and static asset optimization
OWASP ASVS security review
stories.notes: May require external penetration testing

stories.title: Parental consent workflow (COPPA)
stories.notes: Legal review needed; workflow for under-13 edge cases

stories.title: FERPA data handling compliance
stories.notes: Affects data export, retention, access controls

stories.title: Pilot school onboarding
stories.notes: First school will surface all the SSO edge cases

stories.title: Documentation (user guides, admin guides)
stories.notes: Often forgotten; essential for school admin adoption

assumptions: Team has prior experience with school SSO integrations (Google Workspace, Microsoft AAD)
Legal/compliance review for FERPA/COPPA can be completed in parallel with development
At least one pilot school is available and responsive during Phase 5
Design system and UI components can be leveraged from existing library (not built from scratch)
Google and Microsoft API access/approvals do not require extended enterprise review
No payment processing in scope (deferred to future phase)
Stakeholder availability for discovery and feedback does not cause delays

roles.notes: Full-time required given stakeholder coordination and compliance requirements
PWA, real-time updates, accessibility hardening justify two FTEs
Multi-tenant architecture, multiple integrations, compliance requirements
Critical for compliance; integration testing across SSO providers

red_flags: Proposed 24-week timeline is not achievable with stated scope — realistic estimate is 38 weeks
No QA mentioned in brief — compliance requirements demand dedicated QA
No security/compliance specialist mentioned — FERPA/COPPA/OWASP cannot be afterthoughts
Four distinct SSO/calendar/drive integrations (Google + Microsoft × Auth + Files) require significant engineering time
WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all features is substantial — often underestimated by 30-50%
Multi-tenant architecture with data isolation for minors' data requires careful design upfront

risks.risk: School SSO configuration variance — each school's Google Workspace or Microsoft AAD setup differs; first 3-5 schools will surface edge cases requiring custom handling
risks.mitigation: Budget 2 weeks of integration debugging per SSO provider; create detailed SSO setup checklist for school admins; build diagnostic tooling

risks.risk: FERPA/COPPA compliance scope creep — legal review may surface requirements not in current scope (parental consent flows, data retention limits, audit requirements)
risks.mitigation: Engage legal counsel in week 1; block feature development on compliance review sign-off; maintain compliance requirements backlog

risks.risk: Google/Microsoft API approval delays — enterprise API access for Drive/Calendar may require extended security review from Google/Microsoft (4-8 weeks)
risks.mitigation: Submit API access requests in week 1; design integration layer to gracefully degrade; identify which integrations can ship post-MVP

risks.risk: Accessibility remediation cascade — WCAG 2.1 AA audit in Phase 4 may surface systemic issues requiring significant rework of earlier features
risks.mitigation: Integrate accessibility testing into every sprint; use automated a11y testing in CI; conduct mid-project accessibility audit at week 12

risks.risk: Pilot school responsiveness — pilot launch requires active participation from students, advisors, and admins; school schedules (breaks, exams) may cause delays
risks.mitigation: Identify pilot school by week 4; align pilot timing with school calendar; have backup pilot school identified

risks.risk: Real-time collaboration complexity — discussions, Kanban, and notifications with real-time updates significantly increase frontend and infrastructure complexity
risks.mitigation: Evaluate WebSocket vs polling trade-offs early; consider managed service (Pusher, Ably) vs self-hosted; scope real-time to specific features only

risks.risk: Multi-tenant data isolation failure — bug allowing cross-tenant data access would be catastrophic given minor students' data
risks.mitigation: Implement tenant isolation at database level (row-level security or schema separation); mandatory security review of all data access paths; penetration testing before pilot

risks.risk: Gamification scope creep — points/badges system has unbounded complexity; stakeholders will request more features once they see it
risks.mitigation: Define fixed v1 gamification scope (attendance points, 3 badge types); defer leaderboards and custom badges to post-launch

confidence.rationale: The proposed 24-week timeline is approximately 40% shorter than what this scope realistically requires. The combination of FERPA/COPPA compliance for minors, four major third-party integrations (Google/Microsoft × Auth/Files), multi-tenant architecture, and WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility creates compounding complexity. Without confirmed SSO configurations from pilot schools and completed legal review, significant unknowns remain.
what_would_increase_confidence: Complete FERPA/COPPA legal review and document all compliance requirements
Secure pilot school commitment with confirmed SSO provider and admin access
Validate Google Workspace and Microsoft AAD API access timelines
Add dedicated QA and security/compliance specialist to team
Extend timeline to 36-40 weeks or reduce scope (e.g., defer Microsoft integrations to post-launch)
Conduct technical spike on multi-tenant data isolation approach

dimensions.rationale: Brief is detailed but compliance requirements (FERPA/COPPA specifics) and integration details (which SSO providers, which schools) are undefined
dimensions.action: Complete legal compliance review and confirm pilot school SSO configuration before sprint planning

dimensions.rationale: No team specified in brief; estimate assumes 8.5 FTE but actual team composition unknown; no QA or security specialist mentioned
dimensions.action: Confirm team composition; add dedicated QA and security/compliance specialist

dimensions.rationale: Four major integrations (Google SSO, Microsoft SSO, Drive, OneDrive) plus calendar sync; school-specific SSO configs vary widely; API approval timelines unknown
dimensions.action: Submit API access requests immediately; conduct SSO integration spike with actual school tenant in week 1-2

dimensions.rationale: 24-week proposed timeline is not achievable — scope requires 38+ weeks; no buffer for compliance findings or integration debugging
dimensions.action: Extend timeline to 36-40 weeks or explicitly defer integrations and Phase 3 features to post-launch

dimensions.rationale: Key assumptions unverified: SSO experience, legal review timeline, pilot school availability, API approval timelines
dimensions.action: Validate top 3 assumptions (legal timeline, pilot school, API access) before committing to timeline

open_questions: Which schools are confirmed for pilot, and what SSO providers do they use (Google Workspace, Microsoft AAD, or both)?
Has legal counsel reviewed FERPA/COPPA requirements, and are there known compliance blockers for storing minors' data?
Is parental consent required for students under 13, and if so, what is the approved workflow?
What is the actual team composition and their experience with school SSO integrations?
Are Google and Microsoft API access approvals already in progress, or do they need to be initiated?
Is the 24-week timeline a hard constraint (e.g., tied to school year), or can it be extended?
Which features can be deferred to post-launch if timeline is fixed (e.g., Microsoft integrations, task dependencies, gamification)?
Is there an existing design system to leverage, or must components be built from scratch?
What is the expected scale at launch (number of schools, students, concurrent users)?
Is there budget for external security penetration testing before pilot launch?
